message.content:
<deliberation>
Goal: Evaluate each main GDPR node against the provided PwC DE marketing policy chunk. Identify deviations only when the policy text contradicts or narrows the GDPR text (with exact quotes from GDPR and policy). Omissions are Non-Deviation. Include cross-references from the provided referenced_nodes where relevant.

Approach per node:
- REG-036 (Art21(2)): Right to object to direct marketing (incl. profiling). Cross-ref considered: REG-035 (Art21(1)) to understand objection context. Policy clearly offers an “at any time” objection for electronic marketing and states marketing will stop upon objection/withdrawal; it also discloses profiling for marketing under consent. No narrowing found.
- REG-037 (Art21(3)): Upon objection to direct marketing, processing must stop for that purpose. Policy states it will no longer use data for marketing if consent is withdrawn and/or the user objects. No contradiction.
- REG-001 (Art6(1)): Lawful bases. Policy identifies consent for email/direct marketing with profiling and legitimate interests for existing-customer email marketing; no claim that these are the only bases. No contradiction with the catalogue in Art6(1). Omissions (e.g., balancing details) are Non-Deviation.
- REG-080 (Art46(3)): Appropriate safeguards for international transfers subject to authorization. Policy chunk does not address transfers; omissions treated as Non-Deviation. Consider cross-ref REG-078 (Art46(1)) for context only.
- REG-017 (Art14(2)): Information when data not obtained from the data subject (storage period, legitimate interests, rights incl. objection/portability, withdrawal, complaint right, source, ADM under Art22). The marketing chunk does not purport to be a comprehensive Art14 notice for indirectly obtained data; omissions are Non-Deviation. Cross-refs considered: REG-016 (Art14(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-041 (Art22(1)), and main REG-001 for 6(1) references.
- REG-029 (Art18(2)): Handling of data under restriction. The policy does not expressly place data under Art18 restriction; it describes maintaining a suppression/blocking list after objection/withdrawal to prevent future marketing. No statement contradicts the Art18(2) rule; omissions are Non-Deviation. Cross-ref considered: REG-028 (Art18(1)).
- REG-025 (Art17(1)): Right to erasure without undue delay where certain grounds apply (withdrawn consent with no other legal ground; objection under 21(2), etc.). The policy explicitly states retention of certain personal data (for documentation/verification and maintaining a blocking list) beyond revocation/objection, with deletion “generally after three years.” This introduces a specific retention timeline that can delay erasure compared to the “without undue delay” standard when Art17(1)(b) or (c) grounds apply. Even if the controller may rely on another legal ground for that limited retention, the chunk itself sets a general three-year retention following revocation/objection, which departs from the immediacy of Art17(1). Classify as a Time deviation. Cross-refs considered from provided set: REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-035 (Art21(1)), REG-036 (Art21(2) main), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-005 (Art8(1)).

Only REG-025 shows a deviation; others are Non-Deviation.
</deliberation>

```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-036",
    "clause": "Art21(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-035"],
    "deviation_found": false,

  },
  {

  {

  {
    "reg_id": "REG-080",
    "clause": "Art46(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-078"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-016", "REG-009", "REG-041", "REG-001"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-029",
    "clause": "Art18(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-028"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-025",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-035", "REG-036", "REG-009", "REG-005"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Time deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies: ... (b) the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing; ... (c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(1) and there are no overriding legitimate grounds for the processing, or the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(2);",
        "policy_quote": "If you withdraw your consent and/or object to the data processing, PwC DE will no longer use your data for marketing purposes. PwC DE will store the data required for the organisational management of your consent beyond the time of your revocation and/or objection in order to fulfil documentation and verification requirements and to ensure that your data is not processed by PwC DE for marketing purposes in the future (so-called blocking list), unless you provide new consent. PwC DE will delete your data when these organisational purposes cease to apply, which will generally be after a period of three years at the end of the year following the cessation of marketing activity by PwC DE.",
        "mismatch": "GDPR requires erasure without undue delay when consent is withdrawn or the data subject objects to direct marketing, whereas the policy retains certain personal data for generally around three years after revocation/objection before deletion.",
        "analysis": "By setting a general three‑year timeline to delete data retained for documentation/suppression after withdrawal/objection, the policy introduces a fixed delay rather than erasure ‘without undue delay’ where Art17(1)(b)/(c) grounds apply."
      }
    ]
  }
]
```
